title: This week, the Santa Clara County Board of Supervisors approved extending their first-of-its-kind pilot program that provides universal basic income to transition age foster youth.
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The pilot program in Santa Clara County has yielded clear results and inspired a growing number of similar direct cash assistance initiatives across the nation.",
    "Foster youth in this program are reporting that they can use these funds to sustain their basic needs and improve their credit scores.",
    "The Universal Basic Income for Transition Age Foster Youth Act aims to provide unconditional and direct cash assistance of $1,000 a month to approximately 2,500 youth aging out of the Extended Foster Care Program in California.",
    "A recent report on the County's pilot program indicated that respondents reported the funding allowed them to cover basic expenses and save money.",
    "Participants in financial mentorship reported significant increases in their credit scores and a feeling of empowerment regarding their financial resources.",
    "One youth in the program was able to establish and meet a savings goal for a down payment on a house.",
    "The 'Trust Youth Initiative' in New York City will provide 30-40 young adults experiencing homelessness with $1,250 per month for up to 2 years."
  ]
}
```
